goals: Tensor methods have played a central role in analyzing multi-dimensional data. Also, these methods provide low-rank representations, which enable trustworthy and parsimonious machine learning systems. However, tensor methods remain underexplored in the context of modern neural architectures and foundation models. This workshop aims to provide a forum for advancing tensor methods and applying them to various applications at the intersection of data mining and modern machine learning, including foundation models, representation learning, and scientific machine learning.
scope_intro: The topics of interest include, but are not limited to:

scope.description: New tensor decomposition methods, tensor-based representation learning, tensor foundation models, integration of tensor models with foundation models, and pre-training paradigms.
Analysis of tensor structures for interpretability, robustness, and trustworthiness.
Emerging applications of tensor methods in physical AI and scientific data analysis and discovery.
Datasets, benchmarks, and evaluation protocols for reproducibility and new applications.
Any other topics involving tensor methods in machine learning.

after_scope: Accepted papers will be presented as posters, with a subset selected for oral presentations. The workshop will take place in person at KDD 2026, and authors of accepted papers are required to register and attend the conference in person. This workshop is non-archival; accepted papers will not appear in the KDD proceedings.

submission_types.description: Up to 9 pages (excluding references). Original research contributions.
Up to 4 pages (excluding references). Work-in-progress or position papers.

submission_guidelines.label: Formatting
submission_guidelines.text: All submissions must be in PDF format and follow the KDD 2026 formatting guidelines.

submission_guidelines.label: Anonymous
submission_guidelines.text: Papers should be anonymized for double-blind review. All submissions must adhere to the ACM policies on plagiarism, misrepresentation, and falsification.

submission_guidelines.label: Dual Submission
submission_guidelines.text: We welcome both original and previously presented work, including preliminary, ongoing, published, or work currently under review. There are no restrictions on prior publication status.

submission_site: Submit papers through the Submission Portal on OpenReview (link TBD). Papers will remain private during review. All authors must maintain up-to-date OpenReview profiles for conflict-of-interest management and paper matching. Learn how to create an OpenReview profile here.
